text: Meeting deadlines can be stressful, but good time management makes it much easier. Start by breaking a large project into smaller tasks, then set realistic daily goals. Prioritization is key—focus on the most important or urgent tasks first, and consider using a simple to-do list or calendar. Be careful not to take on too much; scope management means understanding what is actually required and saying no to unnecessary extra work. Finally, if you realize you might miss a deadline, be honest and communicate early with your team or client. Explaining the situation and proposing a new timeline usually works better than hiding the problem.
